title: Taiwan expresses concern after China designates island as biggest risk in US-China relations
summary: Taiwan's government responded with concern after Chinese officials characterized the island as the most significant risk factor in US-China bilateral relations, marking an escalation in cross-strait diplomatic rhetoric during a period of heightened military tensions.
contents:
## Taiwan Pushes Back Against China's Diplomatic Framing

### Development
Chinese officials designated Taiwan as the single greatest risk in US-China relations, prompting an immediate response from Taipei's government expressing deep concern over the characterization.

### Strategic Context
- China has intensified diplomatic pressure on Taiwan throughout early 2026
- The PLA conducted expanded naval exercises around Taiwan in recent months
- Cross-strait tensions have escalated alongside the broader US-Iran conflict, which diverted US military attention to the Middle East
- The US redeployed THAAD and Patriot systems from the Far East to support operations against Iran

### Taiwan's Response
Taiwan's government reaffirmed its commitment to maintaining the cross-strait status quo while strengthening self-defense capabilities. Officials emphasized that Taiwan's democratic governance and sovereignty remain non-negotiable.

### Regional Implications
The characterization aligns with Beijing's intensified rhetoric ahead of potential US-China diplomatic engagement. President Trump has indicated his upcoming May visit to China may coincide with discussions on regional security, including the Korean Peninsula.

### Source Attribution
Associated Press reporting via Yahoo News World section.
